title: Neon Saints of the Machine
genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
concept: Hymns sung by people who work night shifts inside systems built to replace them — a sacred technology where the machine is the cathedral and the congregation is whoever is still clocked in at 3am.
statement: Industrial textures and broken beats held together by gospel call-and-response and real jazz harmony underneath — not 'cyberpunk' as costume, but the actual ache of staying human inside something built to be efficient.
visualIdentity: Wet concrete, sodium and neon reflections, server-room cathedrals, work gloves and stained-glass made of broken phone screens.
typographyMood: Sharp, mono-spaced, slightly glitched at the edges — uppercase labels lit like exit signs, italic serif only for the gospel responses.
musicalStyle: Dark cyber-soul, industrial DnB, gospel shadows, jazz harmony, elegant hard electronic pulse. Aggressive smooth bass, broken beats, industrial textures, jazz piano, brass shadows, distorted choir, analog synths, metallic percussion.
vocalDirection: Deep wounded male or androgynous low vocal, soulful but controlled — restraint under pressure, never shouted.
